intro.title: Vector Addition
paragraphs: To add vectors, connect them head-to-tail.
Below, A = ⟨3, 0⟩ and B = ⟨0, 2⟩ combine to give A + B = ⟨3, 2⟩.

segments.value: We can find the result by adding the x-components and y-components separately:
Let's try some more complicated problems.

questions.prompt: Here a = ⟨−1, 0⟩ and b = ⟨0, 3⟩. Drag vector b so its tail meets the head of vector a (head-to-tail), then enter where the path a + b ends.
questions.hint: Look at the a + b vector from the origin to where the path ends. Count how far it goes right or left (the first number) and how far up or down (the second number).
questions.explanation: To add vectors, we connect them head-to-tail. Start by following vector a, then continue along vector b. The path ends at ⟨−1, 0⟩ + ⟨0, 3⟩ = ⟨−1, 3⟩, which is a + b.

questions.prompt: Here a = ⟨3, 0⟩ and b = ⟨0, 4⟩ are already connected head-to-tail. Draw a + b from the origin to the end of the path, then enter what a + b is.
questions.hint: Look only at the a + b arrow. From the origin, count how far it goes right or left (the first number) and how far up or down (the second number).
questions.explanation: a + b is the arrow from the start of the path to its end. ⟨3, 0⟩ + ⟨0, 4⟩ = ⟨3 + 0, 0 + 4⟩ = ⟨3, 4⟩.

questions.prompt: Here a = ⟨0, 3⟩ and b = ⟨3, 0⟩ both start at the origin. Align them head-to-tail, draw a + b, then enter what a + b is.
questions.hint: Once aligned, look only at the a + b arrow. From the origin, count how far it goes right or left (the first number) and how far up or down (the second number).
questions.explanation: Connect a and b head-to-tail, then a + b is the arrow from the start to the end of the path. ⟨0, 3⟩ + ⟨3, 0⟩ = ⟨0 + 3, 3 + 0⟩ = ⟨3, 3⟩.

questions.prompt: Here a = ⟨1, −2⟩ and b = ⟨2, 3⟩ both start at the origin. Align them head-to-tail, draw a + b, then enter what a + b is.
questions.hint: Once aligned, look only at the a + b arrow. From the origin, count how far it goes right or left (the first number) and how far up or down (the second number).
questions.explanation: Connect a and b head-to-tail, then a + b is the arrow from the start to the end of the path. ⟨1, −2⟩ + ⟨2, 3⟩ = ⟨1 + 2, −2 + 3⟩ = ⟨3, 1⟩.

questions.prompt: Now try it on your own! Here a = ⟨−2, 1⟩ and b = ⟨3, 2⟩. Move the vectors to find the path, then enter what a + b is.
questions.hint: Look at the a + b arrow from the origin: count how far it goes right or left (the first number) and how far up or down (the second number).
questions.explanation: Connect a and b head-to-tail, then a + b is the arrow from the start to the end of the path. ⟨−2, 1⟩ + ⟨3, 2⟩ = ⟨−2 + 3, 1 + 2⟩ = ⟨1, 3⟩.

questions.prompt: Now try it on your own! Here a = ⟨3, −1⟩ and b = ⟨−1, 3⟩. Move the vectors to find the path, then enter what a + b is.
questions.hint: Look at the a + b arrow from the origin: count how far it goes right or left (the first number) and how far up or down (the second number).
questions.explanation: Connect a and b head-to-tail, then a + b is the arrow from the start to the end of the path. ⟨3, −1⟩ + ⟨−1, 3⟩ = ⟨3 + (−1), −1 + 3⟩ = ⟨2, 2⟩.
